what_it_does: This file records a proposed schema for an editable location (edit_target) that tells an agent exactly where to make a change, distinguishing content-level targets, UI-level targets, and their resolved code symbols, plus a dependencies list drawn from code-archaeology.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id item-7abbe24d, address board://item-7abbe24d, type block, source claude_code, and a part_of link to board://item-e42d651f.
Title is 'P10 · --- the editable location (Tim's "reduce the agent's effort") ---'.
Defines edit_target with three sub-fields: content_mode, ui_mode, and dependencies.
ui_mode is ui://<kind>/<ref> → code://<stem>/<symbol>, described as 'the REAL join'.
The resolver is named Suite.resolve_scope and reads design/_system/{addresses,code-symbols}.json.
dependencies are expected to be [code://…] values from code-archaeology cards (imports/declares).
Created and updated timestamps are identical; history shows a single 'filed' transition.
standouts: The 'edit_target' concept is framed as direct addressability to reduce agent effort.
The UI-to-code mapping is delegated to Suite.resolve_scope reading design/_system JSON files.
Dependencies are explicitly tied to code-archaeology findings.
conventions: Uses ledger item YAML frontmatter plus a markdown body.
Uses URI-style identifiers: board://, file://, ui://, code://.
References external system files under design/_system/.
concerns: No concrete example of an edit_target is provided.
Suite.resolve_scope and the design/_system JSON files are referenced but not defined in this file.
content_mode and ui_mode appear overlapping; the intended precedence is not stated.
All dependency and symbol arrays are empty or unspecified.
notes: A design note proposing how to point an agent at the exact editable place by resolving UI references to code symbols and attaching code-archaeology dependencies.
questions: What is the implementation and interface of Suite.resolve_scope?
Where do design/_system/addresses.json and design/_system/code-symbols.json live and what schema do they use?
How are dependencies populated automatically from code-archaeology cards?
What does 'P10' signify in the title?
When should content_mode be used versus ui_mode?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to define the editable location to reduce agent effort; the file does that at a schematic level, matching the purpose but without examples or implementation detail.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a substrate-level addressing scheme so that agents can receive precise, resolvable edit targets rather than having to search for where to change.
novelty: The combination of a UI reference resolved to a code symbol via a dedicated resolver, plus dependencies sourced from code-archaeology cards, as a single edit-target record.
contribution: Provides an edit-target addressing model and a UI-to-code resolution hook that a fused system could retain to make agent edits precise and traceable.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger item that proposes an edit_target addressing scheme mapping UI references to code symbols via Suite.resolve_scope and listing code-archaeology-derived dependencies.
intention_for_embedding: To reduce agent effort by giving every intended edit a direct, resolvable location instead of requiring the agent to search for it.
